Title: The Queens of Animation: The Untold Story of the Women Who Transformed the World of Disney and Made Cinematic History
Author: Nathalia Holt
Comment: If you are at all into the art of Disney animation this book was a well researched  look at the unsung female artists at the studio.

Title: A Tale for the Time Being
Author: Ruth Ozeki

Title: The Dangers of Smoking in Bed
Author: Mariana Enríquez
Comment: Quick read, often funny, always unsettling

Title: Glass Houses (Chief Inspector Armand Gamache #13)
Author: Louise Penny
Comment: Y’all I know so many of us read this series but this one absolutely had me on the edge of my seat! If you don’t know Inspector Gamache or the charming Three Pines you really should start at the beginning.

Title: Will my cat eat my eyeballs? And other questions about dead bodies
Author: Caitlin doughty

Title: Nathan Hale’s Hazardous Tales Raid of No Return
Author: Nathan Hale

Title: So Late in the Day
Author: Claire Keegan
Genre: Collection of short stories! I’m counting it!

Title: A Letter to the Luminous Deep (The Sunken Archive #1)
Author: Sylvie Cathrall
Comment: This conceit was interesting to me but the epistolary form had the author tying themselves in knots to tidy the ending. At over 400 pages this read was more frustrating than diverting.

Title: Stiff: The curious life of human cadavers
Author: Mary Roach

Title: West with the Night
Author: Beryl Markham
Comment: Beryl is a dear old friend. This is a reread for me and even anticipating her turns of phrase I was still gobsmacked by her prose. On oft overlooked classic, read it, and then wash it down with Paula McClain’s Circling the Sun. You won’t be disappointed.

Title: Winter's Orbit
Author: Everina Maxwell
Comment: Delightful!

Title: A Memory of Light
Author: Robert Jordan & Brandon Sanderson
Comment: A two year payoff and what a way to go

Title: Sun House
Author: David James Duncan
Comment: This one won’t be for everyone. Honestly, there’s a version of me that this book isn’t for. But it hit me at the right moment and the message (though overwrought and downright exhaustingly repeated at times) is quite powerful.

Title: The Committed
Author: Viet Thanh Nguyen
Comment: The sympathizer was better

Title: Gulp: Adventures on the Alimentary Canal
Author: Mary Roach

Title: Attachments
Author: Rainbow Rowell
Comment: I’m working through Rowell’s back list. She fables in many genres this happens to be an epistolary contemporary romance set to the back drop of Y2K. It was a true delight! Her voice is always witty and she manages to approach personal traumas with tender care. My favorite will always be her fantasy Simon Snow trilogy but I love her in the romcom sphere as well.

Title: The Heiress
Author: Rachel Hawkins
Comment: Fun read, couldn't put it down!

Title: Ain't I a Woman: Black Women and Feminism
Author: bell hooks
Comment: Incredibly well researched, and groundbreaking for its time. Occasionally tedious, but only because these ideas are now fully mainstream as third-wave and intersectional feminism

Title: Trespasses
Author: Louise Kennedy
Comment: Long listed for the Booker, short listed for the Women’s Prize, and absolutely deserving of both. Trespasses is set in Northern Ireland during the troubles, Cushla is thrust between keeping her head down in her Catholic enclave or following a forbidden passion. This book has a moment that had me gasping. Can’t wait to read more from Kennedy.

Title: Iris Kelly Doesn’t Date
Author: Ashley Herring Blake
Comment: Cute queer romance. ffwd through the sexy time.

Title: Songteller
Author: Dolly Parton
Comment: She is so compelling, and I could listen to her talk about anything forever, so it covers up the roughness of this so-called book lol. It’s basically snippets of interviews where she reflects on songs and her life in no particular order.

Title: The Last Murder at the End of the World
Author: Stuart Turton

Title: Forensics: What Bugs, Burns, Prints, DNA and More Tell Us About Crime
Author: Val McDermid

Title: Prophet song
Author: Paul Lynch
Comment:
Booker prize '23
Beautiful writing but harrowing, bleak, etc

Title: Lincoln in the Bardo
Author: George Saunders
Comment: An interesting exploration of the liminal space we out occupy after death. This was a particularly compelling read due in large part to the inventive full cast audio production.

Title: Lincoln in the Bardo
Author: George Saunders
Comment: Good poop jokes

Title: A Woman of No Importance
Author: Sonia Purnell
Comment: Spy shit

Title: The Book of Charlie: Wisdom from the Remarkable American Life of a 109-Year-Old Man
Author: David von Drehle
Comment: This was a diverting if not particularly cogent look at a 109 year old’s perseverance through an ever changing world. von Drehle attempts to weave in some pop-psychology around stoicism but Charlie is interesting enough to forgive it. I am unlikely to really ponder much of anything from this book again.

Title: The Wind's Twelve Quarters
Author: Ursula K. Le Guin
Comment: Short stories galore.

Title: I’ve Never Met an Idiot on the River
Author: Henry Winkler

Title: The Last Murder at the End of the World
Author: Stuart Turton

Title: Funny Story
Author: Emily Henry
Comment: Easily the best execution of the “fake dating” I’ve read. Henry continues to up her game. I dare say I enjoyed this even more than Book People.

Title: Botanical Curses and Poisons: The Shadow-Lives of Plants
Author: Fez Inkwright

Title: The Light in the Ruins
Author: Chris Bohjalian
Comment: This dual timeline historical fiction is a good summer read. The story unfolds just prior to the liberation of Italy in WWII at a Marchese’s villa that entertains and later houses German soldiers. In 1955 someone is slowly murdering members of the Marchese’s surviving family and the investigator assigned to the case must unravel her own connection to the Florentine villa. It’s a very quick read, good not great.

Title: Attachments
Author: Rainbow Rowell
Comment: Lovely palate cleanser after several bleak reads!

Title: Death in Glacier National Park: Stories of Accidents and Foolhardiness in the Crown of the Continent
Author: Randi Minetor
Comment: Wear a life jacket, watch your step, and never hike alone. For best results, avoid being a teenage boy.

Title: Victory City
Author: Salman Rushdie
Comment: A masterpiece

Title: I Don’t Want To Die Poor
Author: Michael Arceneaux

Title: The Lost Bookshop
Author: Evie Woods
Comment: An ode to books, wrapped in a mystery surrounded by a bit of magic. This charming story is told in dual timelines and while doled out slowly it is compulsively readable.  Fine summer fun!

Title: Do No Harm: Stories of Life, Death, and Brain Surgery
Author: Henry Marsh

Title: Us, After: A Memoir of Love and Suicide
Author: Rachel Zimmerman

Title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
Author: TJ Klune

Title: Holy City
Author: Henry Wise
Comment: Do not read this trash. Seriously, it’s wrong in so many, many ways. I’m not giving it 1* because at least it is what it says on the tin; it’s just a terrible version of it.

Title: Wicked: The Life and Times of the Wicked Witch of the West
Author: Gregory Maguire

Title: Love and Other Flight Delays
Author: Denise Williams
Comment: These three romance novellas are loosely linked and quick reads. Ideal for the airport or any other summer travel plans.

Title: I Hope This Finds You Well
Author: Natalie Sue

Title: The Six Deaths of the Saint
Author: Alix E. Harrow

Title: The September House
Author: Carissa Orlando
Comment: Excellent horror, very fun. Not for the faint of heart!

Title: The Last Devil to Die
Author: Richard Osman
Comment: Part of Thursday Murder Club series

Title: Earthlings
Author: Sayaka Murata
Comment:
So many warnings: rape, incest, child molestation, grooming, murder, cannibalism.

Weirdest book I've read in so long.

Title: A Witch’s Guide to Fake Dating a Demon
Author: Sarah Hawley

Title: Ward D
Author: Freida McFadden
Comment: Not good at all.

Title: Winterland
Author: Rae Meadows
Comment: This was a great read leading up to the Summer Olympics! Winterland is set in 70s Soviet Union around a gymnastic training school. It’s a times a difficult read but a fantastic glimpse into the Russian sports machine.

Title: Soulless
Author: Gail Carriger
Comment: Great fun. Kinda creepy, kinda spicy.

Title: The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes
Author: Suzanne Collins

Title: The Boys in the Boat: Nine Americans and Their Epic Quest for Gold at the 1936 Berlin Olympics
Author: Daniel James Brown
Comment: This is a great narrative non-fiction read especially leading up to the Olympics. The last chapter will have you on the edge of your seat despite knowing the outcome. BRB going to look up archival footage!

Title: All the Colors of the Dark
Author: Chris Whitaker

Title: Magic Treehouse: Polar Bears Past Bedtime
Author: Mary Pope Osborne

Title: The 100-Year-Old Man Who Climbed Out The Window And Disappeared
Author: Jonas Jonasson
Comment: Freaking delightful!!

Title: A Dawn with the the Wolf Knight
Author: Elise Kova

Title: On Looking: Eleven Walks With Expert Eyes
Author: Alexandra Horowitz

Title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
Author: Mark Manson
Comment: Meh

Title: The Games: A Global History of the Olympics
Author: David Goldblatt
Comment: This is a fascinating look at the modern games at the intersection of geopolitics and sport. Spoiler; it’s not all celebrations and gold medals.

Title: God Bless You, Otis Spunkmeyer
Author: Joseph Earl Thomas
Comment: Outstanding.

Title: The Boys in the Boat
Author: Daniel James Brown
Comment: If you like descriptions of rowing this is the book for you

Title: Slow Dance
Author: Rainbow Rowell
Comment: Rowell is charming as ever in an adult romance circling around two childhood best friends seeking second chances. This gave me some big nostalgic feels.

Title: Aru Shah and the End of Time
Author: Roshani Chokshi

Title: The Babysitters Club Boy Crazy Stacy
Author: Ann M. Martin

Title: The Babysitters Club Logan Likes Mary Anne!
Author: Ann M. Martin

Title: The Babysitters Club Claudia and the Bad Joke
Author: Ann M. Martin

Title: The Babysitters Club The Truth About Stacey
Author: Ann M. Martin

Title: The Babysitters Club Kristy’s Great Idea
Author: Ann M. Martin

Title: Dork Diaries: Tales from a Not-So-Posh Paris Adventure
Author: Rachel Renee Russell

Title: Dork Diaries: Tales from a Not-So-Dorky Drama Queen
Author: Rachel Renee Russell

Title: Dork Diaries: Tales from a Not-So-Graceful Ice Princess
Author: Rachel Renee Russell

Title: Dork Diaries: Tales from a Not-So-Best Friend Forever
Author: Rachel Renee Russell

Title: The Mysterious Benedict Society and the Perilous Journey
Author: Trenton Lee Stewart

Title: Magic Treehouse: Polar Bears Past Bedtime
Author: Mary Pope Osborne

Title: Lenny Marks Gets Away with Murder
Author: Kerry Mayne

Title: Dork Diaries: Tales from a Not-So-Happy Birthday
Author: Rachel Renee Russell

Title: Sandwich
Author: Catherine Newman
Comment: Laugh out loud funny and heart wrenching and beautiful. Narrator and characters are very similar to We All Want Impossible Things, in a different setting and plot.

Title: A Lady’s Guide to Fortune Hunting
Author: Sophie Irwin

Title: The Overstory
Author: Richard Powers
Comment:
Thanks to everyone who recommended it!
Page count on my Kobo was 700+
